repo_truth: The 40-path pre-dispatch subject contains the complete accumulated HCM control-pack work plus the HCM-0.8 correction. The immutable HCM-0.1 history demonstrates a user-routed review/remediation queue. The active contract now intends one parent-owned slice loop, internal built-in default subagents, JSON dispatches, v1.2 true-stop handoffs, and two-commit closeout. No Rust files are in scope.
allowed_scope: Read the complete 40-path subject identified by subject_fingerprint.
Run read-only schema, validator, link, consistency, archive, portability, and docs-only checks.
Report findings only; do not edit, commit, write a handoff, append the ledger, or create another dispatch.
non_goals: No changes to immutable v1.0/v1.1 records or legacy Markdown dispatches.
No review of future Handbook runtime implementation.
No global handoff or ledger write by the reviewer.
tasks: Independently verify that 00 through 08 define one long-lived explicit phase/slice parent rather than a next-session queue.
Verify internal dispatch ownership, required skills, built-in default/fresh agent constraints, result collection, review-remediation-different-fresh-review lineage, and genuine stop conditions.
Verify v1.2 and internal-dispatch schemas/templates/validator enforce the documented contract without rewriting historical schemas or artifacts.
Verify HCM-0.8 preserves the truthful HCM-0.1 history and does not retroactively claim the new workflow was previously proven.
Run the named validation wall and return findings first with severity, file/line, violated contract/gate, smallest remediation, and missing proof.
stop_conditions: Return blocked to the parent if required files cannot be read or validation infrastructure is unavailable.
Do not widen into runtime implementation or edit any file.
Do not create a handoff or ledger entry.
